H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 shows strong confidence results (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78). Distribution peaks at 8-9 with unimodal shape. Qualitative feedback indicates noticeable improvement in 41% of respondents (12/29), with most settling within 5-10 minutes and high music appreciation (69% liked/loved it). YouTube reach is modest (30 views) with no retention data, suggesting limited discovery. AutoGluon predicts a lower score (6.859) for the overstimulation zone, possibly indicating the model under-captures real-world effectiveness. Panel note: The protocol's strong outcome despite low visibility suggests a high-efficacy asset worthy of targeted outreach.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This protocol helps users feel calmer and more focused, especially during activities like commuting or relaxation, with most reporting noticeable benefits within 10 minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 demonstrates strong confidence (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73). Distribution shows moderate concentration around 7-8. Qualitative data reveals noticeable improvement in 39% (10/21) with variable settle times (some immediate) and mixed music reception. YouTube data is sparse (5 views) with no retention metrics. AutoGluon overstimulation prediction (6.859) closely matches observed mean, suggesting good model alignment. No significant anomalies reported across analysts.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Users report a calming effect from this protocol, though many discover it through limited online visibility.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 presents meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68). Distribution is relatively uniform with slight elevation at 6-8. Qualitative feedback indicates noticeable improvement in 34% (10/29) and frequent use for sleep onset (8/29). YouTube reach is minimal (8 views) with no retention data. AutoGluon prediction (6.859) is slightly below observed mean. Analysts note H-OS-L3's analytical isolation in embedding clusters, suggesting unique response patterns warranting further study.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol supports relaxation and sleep onset, though it remains relatively undiscovered online.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow

Qualitative data indicates noticeable to significant improvement in 46% (20/43) and frequent use in therapy/care-giving contexts. YouTube reach is very low (7 views) with no retention data. AutoGluon anxiety prediction (7.838) exceeds observed mean, suggesting room for optimization. No significant anomalies reported across analysts.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Users feel less stressed after about ten minutes, particularly when using it for therapy or support roles.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock

H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Sleep results are mixed: some users benefit greatly while others notice little change.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series demonstrate comparable central efficacy with meaningful variability, reflecting their distinct therapeutic designs.
• HEALING and THRIVING show similar mean outcomes (~6.9-7.0) with moderate consistency across protocols.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits greater outcome variance and lower mean, aligned with its ascending-arc intent for low-affect states.
• Most protocols reach 'meaningful' confidence thresholds; 'developing' labels primarily reflect limited sample sizes (n<50).
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series expresses efficacy patterns consistent with its intended therapeutic arc, with notable domain-specific variations.
• HEALING protocols deliver reliable regulation effects across stress, crisis, overstimulation, and sleep domains.
• THRIVING cognitive protocols (e.g., focus, creative flow) achieve higher peak outcomes than other subtypes.
• TRANSFORMING ascent protocols display the widest outcome spread, with some exceeding expectations despite lower mean.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns, suggesting potential design or population mismatches.
• X-MC-L3: Low mean (5.70), high variance (2.45), and 50% positive response — anomalous for a mind-clarity descent.
• T-FC-L3: Bimodal response distribution (peaks at 7 and 10) despite high mean (7.63), indicating subgroup effects.
• X-MD-L1/X-MD-L2: Sub-50% positive response rates (45%, 43%) suggest limited efficacy in motivation ascent protocols.
• These patterns warrant investigation into protocol design nuances or listener subgroup characteristics.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements could enhance protocol efficacy and address observed response patterns.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35) – High performer with bimodal response; test L2 variants to balance subgroup differences.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10) – Low mean and high variance; consider extending hold phase or adjusting Hz sweep to improve consistency.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95) – Strong outcome but low YouTube reach (30 views); boost discoverability via curated playlists or algorithmic optimization.
• H-SL-L1 (n=54) – Analytical drift across runs; run A/B tests to stabilize protocol interpretation and reduce variability.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analyses reveal analytical instability in key protocols alongside consistent design clustering.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Multiple protocols (e.g., X-MD-L3 at 0.507, H-SL-L1 at 0.530) show similarity <0.70, indicating potential shifts in listener response patterns or analytical variability.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: High similarity pairs (T-FC-L1 ↔ T-FC-L2 at 0.878, H-SL-L1 ↔ H-SL-L2 at 0.869) validate design consistency; H-OS-L3 remains analytically isolated (mean_sim=0.677).
• Confidence in drift analysis is MODERATE due to limited run history (2-4 observations per protocol), but clustering patterns are robust and reproducible.

by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: This protocol helps users feel calmer and more focused, especially during activities like commuting or relaxation, with most reporting noticeable benefits within 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Users report a calming effect from this protocol, though many discover it through limited online visibility.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: This protocol supports relaxation and sleep onset, though it remains relatively undiscovered online.

by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Sleep results are mixed: some users benefit greatly while others notice little change.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: This protocol helps with sleep for many users, though some report no effect.
